confidentialData.currentDraft:
I, Rahul Verma, hereby serve upon you, DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd, this Formal Legal Notice regarding a significant breach of contract and resultant financial loss. This notice is served under the provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS) and relevant principles of Indian Contract Law.

1. **Subject Matter:** This notice pertains to the failure of DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd to provide wedding photography and videography services as agreed upon, resulting in substantial financial damages. The agreement, implicitly formed through your acceptance of the advance payment, constitutes a legally binding contract.

2. **Breach of Contract and Relevant Sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita:** You, DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd, have demonstrably breached the terms of our agreement. Specifically, you failed to render the contracted services on the agreed-upon date, 2025-11-15. This constitutes a violation of Section 318 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, which deals with ‘Cheating’ (Dagdi).  The act of accepting an advance payment for services you subsequently failed to provide, coupled with the deliberate avoidance of communication (as evidenced by the switched-off phone), clearly indicates an intention to deceive and cause financial harm.  Furthermore, the failure to perform the agreed-upon service can be construed as an attempt to defraud, potentially falling under Section 420 of the Indian Penal Code (IPC) – ‘Cheating’ – although the BNS provides a more contemporary framework for addressing such conduct. We intend to pursue this matter under the BNS.

3. **Details of the Agreement and Payment:** On 2025-10-01, I, Rahul Verma, made a payment of INR 500,000 to DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd as advance payment for your services, as per our verbal agreement and subsequent confirmation email (a copy of which is attached as Exhibit A). The agreed-upon delivery date for the photography and videography services was 2025-11-15.  The payment was made in good faith, relying on your representation of your ability to fulfill the contract.

4. **Description of the Incident:** On the wedding day, 2025-11-15, the photographer assigned to our event failed to appear. Despite repeated attempts to contact you via telephone, your phone remained switched off, and no explanation for your absence was provided. This complete lack of communication and failure to perform the contracted services is wholly unacceptable and constitutes a grave dereliction of your contractual obligations.

5. **Damages:** As a direct consequence of your breach of contract, I have suffered significant financial losses. These losses include, but are not limited to: the loss of the advance payment of INR 500,000, the disruption to the wedding celebrations, and the emotional distress caused by your unprofessional conduct. I am quantifying the damages at INR 500,000, which I demand to be reimbursed immediately.

6. **Demand for Redressal:** I hereby demand the immediate payment of INR 500,000 to compensate for the damages suffered.  This payment should be made within fourteen (14) days from the date of receipt of this notice. Failure to comply with this demand within the stipulated timeframe will leave me with no option but to initiate legal proceedings against DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd in the competent court of law, seeking not only the recovery of the aforementioned sum but also punitive damages and legal costs.

7. **Notice of Intention to File Suit:** Please be advised that if the aforementioned sum is not received within fourteen (14) days, I intend to file a civil suit against DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, seeking full redressal of my claims.  We will also explore all available legal avenues to recover our losses, including seeking a declaration of your breach of contract and an injunction to prevent any further unlawful actions.

8. **Proof of Service:** This notice is served upon you by registered post with acknowledgment due. A copy of this notice is retained for my records.

Sincerely,

Rahul Verma
[Address]
[Contact Number]

confidentialData.currentDraft:
I, Rahul Verma, hereby serve upon you, DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd, this formal legal notice dated 2025-11-16, regarding your gross negligence and breach of contract in failing to provide wedding photography and videography services as agreed upon. The details are enumerated as follows:

1. On or about 2025-10-01, I entered into an agreement with you for the provision of professional photography and videography services for my wedding scheduled on 2025-11-15 at the Taj Hotel, New Delhi. Pursuant to this agreement, I paid you an advance payment of INR 50,000 to secure your services.

2. The terms of our agreement were explicitly confirmed by you via WhatsApp communication on or before the wedding day, wherein you unequivocally stated: 'Yes absolutely. Our team will be there at 8am. Dont worry.' This constituted a binding assurance of your timely and professional delivery of services.

3. Despite the clear confirmation and my advance payment, on the scheduled wedding day, i.e., 2025-11-15, your team failed to arrive at the Taj Hotel at the agreed-upon time of 8:00 AM. Attempts to contact you commenced at 9:00 AM, as evidenced by my WhatsApp messages: 'Where are you guys? It is 9am. Please respond.' and 'Please pick up. This is very unprofessional.' However, your phone remained switched off, and you did not respond to any of my communications.

4. Your deliberate non-appearance and subsequent avoidance of communication constitute a grave breach of contract under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, and amount to cheating under Section 318 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (BNS), as you dishonestly induced me to part with money without any intention of performing the promised service.

5. The failure to provide the agreed-upon services has caused me immense mental agony, distress, and financial loss, including the non-recoverable expenses incurred in planning the wedding around your services and the irreplaceable loss of cherished wedding memories.

6. Therefore, I hereby demand that you forthwith refund the entire advance payment of INR 50,000 within fifteen (15) days from the receipt of this notice. The payment shall be made via bank transfer to my account details: A/C No. XXXXXXXXXX, IFSC Code XXXXXXXXXX, Bank Name XYZ Bank, Branch New Delhi.

7. In the event of your failure to comply with this demand within the stipulated timeframe, I shall be constrained to initiate appropriate civil proceedings for recovery of the amount, compensation for damages, and criminal proceedings under Section 318 of the BNS for cheating. You shall also be liable for all legal costs, expenses, and incidental charges incurred in connection with such proceedings.

8. This notice is issued without prejudice to my other rights and remedies available under law. Any further communication in this matter should be directed to my residential address at [Your Full Address], New Delhi.


Served this 16th day of November, 2025.


Signature: ________________________
Rahul Verma

confidentialData.currentDraft:
I, Rahul Verma, hereby serve upon you, DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd, having its registered office at [Insert Registered Office Address of DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd], and Mr. [Insert Name of Director/Authorized Representative of DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd, if known, otherwise state 'the Proprietor/Managing Director'], this Formal Legal Notice through registered post with acknowledgment due, and simultaneously by email to [Insert Email Address of DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd], for your immediate attention and necessary action.

1. **Subject Matter:** This notice pertains to a breach of contract and fraudulent conduct concerning wedding photography and videography services agreed upon between myself, Rahul Verma, and DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd.

2. **Background and Agreement:** On or about October 1, 2025, I, Rahul Verma, paid an advance sum of INR 50,000 (Indian Rupees Fifty Thousand) to DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd for comprehensive photography and videography services for my wedding, scheduled to take place on November 16, 2025, at the Taj Hotel, [Insert Taj Hotel Location]. This payment constituted a significant portion of the total agreed-upon fee, and was made in reliance upon DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd’s assurances of professional service delivery.

3. **Breach of Contract and Failure to Perform:** As per our agreement, the services were to be rendered on November 16, 2025, commencing at 8:00 AM. However, DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd and its team failed to appear at the designated location and time. Despite repeated attempts to contact your representatives, your phones remained switched off, demonstrating a blatant disregard for the contractual obligations and causing significant distress and disruption to my wedding celebrations.

4. **Evidence of Assurance and Subsequent Non-Performance:** I possess compelling evidence in the form of WhatsApp communication demonstrating DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd’s explicit confirmation of the shoot. Specifically, on November 15, 2025, your representative responded to my query stating, "Yes absolutely. Our team will be there at 8am. Don't worry."  This assurance was relied upon by me in good faith. Subsequently, when I contacted you at 9:00 AM on November 16, 2025, to inquire about the team’s whereabouts, I received no response. Further attempts to reach you proved futile, as your phone was switched off. This constitutes a clear and demonstrable breach of the contractual agreement.

5. **Legal Provisions Invoked – Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property:** Your actions, specifically accepting the advance payment of INR 50,000 and then failing to provide the agreed-upon services, coupled with the deliberate disconnection of communication, constitute the offence of cheating and dishonestly inducing the delivery of property as defined under Section 318 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), 2023.  The act of inducing me to pay the advance by falsely assuring the provision of services falls squarely within the purview of this section. Furthermore, your conduct may also attract provisions under Section 420 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860, for deception.

6. **Legal Provisions Invoked – Breach of Contract:** Your failure to perform the agreed-upon services constitutes a material breach of the contractual agreement. I am entitled to seek damages for the losses and expenses incurred as a direct result of your breach. This includes, but is not limited to, the advance payment of INR 50,000, and any additional expenses incurred in securing alternative photography/videography services at short notice.

7. **Demand for Restitution and Damages:** I hereby demand the following:
    a) Immediate restitution of the advance payment of INR 50,000 (Indian Rupees Fifty Thousand) within fourteen (14) days from the date of receipt of this notice.
    b) Compensation for the distress, inconvenience, and disruption caused to my wedding celebrations due to your failure to perform. The amount of compensation will be determined based on the extent of the damages suffered, and I reserve the right to claim further damages in a subsequent legal proceeding.

8. **Notice of Intention to Pursue Legal Remedies:** Please be advised that if you fail to comply with the demands outlined in this notice within the stipulated timeframe of fourteen (14) days, I shall be compelled to initiate appropriate legal proceedings against DreamShot Studio Pvt Ltd and Mr. [Insert Name of Director/Authorized Representative, if known] before the competent court of law, seeking restitution of the advance payment, compensation for damages, and any other relief deemed just and equitable by the court.  I will also pursue all available remedies, including but not limited to, filing a complaint with the relevant consumer protection forums.

9. **Without Prejudice Clause:** This notice is issued without prejudice to any other rights or remedies available to me, either under law or equity.

10. **Acknowledgement of Receipt:** Kindly acknowledge receipt of this notice within seven (7) days of its dispatch.

Date: [Insert Date]

Rahul Verma
[Insert Full Address of Rahul Verma]
[Insert Contact Number of Rahul Verma]
[Insert Email Address of Rahul Verma]
